mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.50% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 0.50% and remains below that level for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations over a 60-day forward horizon.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 404,596M and HOUST at 708k as of 2017-11
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average by more than 0.5 index points
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following trigger are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.53 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16 as of 2017-11
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand and earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to stronger growth.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating above 1.5% year-over-year combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 1.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 150k
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.29 and PAYEMS at 133,088k as of 2017-11
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between production and employment growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
